sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
seg = current_frame.segmentation

# Check white/light gray pixel position
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] in ['W', 'w']:
        b = n['boundary']
        print(f"Pixel color={n['color']}: pixels={n['pixels']}, boundary={b}")

# Check if blue cross moved
blue_free = [n for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'b']
charcoal_children = set()
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'c':
        charcoal_children.update(n['children'])
blue_cross = [n for n in blue_free if n['id'] not in charcoal_children]
print(f"\nBlue cross nodes: {len(blue_cross)}")
for n in blue_cross:
    print(f"  pixels={n['pixels']}, boundary={n['boundary'][:4]}")

# Check orange X
orange_free = [n for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'O' and n['id'] not in charcoal_children]
print(f"\nOrange X pixels: {len(orange_free)}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

So cc = 12 and cr ≤ 3. Then R = (3-cr) + (21-12) = 12 - cr.

cc - cr - 18 = cc - cr ✓ (always true)
So R = cc - cr - 18 for cr ≤ 3 and cc ≥ 27.

So all 3 dots are on a diamond with cr ≤ 3 and cc ≥ 27, with R = cc - cr - 18.
The diamond radius is 9, so R = 9.
cc - cr - 18 = 9 → cc - cr = 27
With cr ≤ 3 and cc ≥ 27:

35 = 4*R → R = 8.75, not an integer.
